title: [test] CLI --version flag output and exit code
description:
Integration test for the --version flag (feature gh-toy-4ef).

Framework: Jest. Import CLI dispatch and capture stdout + exit code (or spawn via child_process).

File to create: tests/cli-version.test.ts

Assertions:
- 'noteapi --version' writes a line matching /fleet-e2e-toy v\d+\.\d+\.\d+/ (expect exactly 'fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0' for current package.json) to stdout and exits 0.
- 'noteapi -v' behaves identically.
- Version flag exits 0 even when combined with another flag (e.g. 'noteapi --version --json').

Done when: 'npm test' runs this file and all cases pass.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=S model=claude-haiku-4-5 doer_tokens=600 reviewer_tokens=264 output_usd=NaN

title: [test] CLI help output and input validation
description:
Integration test for the CLI help system and input validation (feature gh-toy-7rp).

Framework: Jest. Import CLI handlers/index dispatch and capture stdout/stderr and exit code (or spawn via child_process against PORT=3001).

File to create: tests/cli-help-validation.test.ts

Assertions:
- 'noteapi --help' and 'noteapi -h' print usage containing all subcommand names and exit 0.
- 'noteapi create --help' prints create-specific usage and exits 0.
- Unknown command exits non-zero and prints a usage hint to stderr (no stack trace).
- create/read/update with an empty or whitespace-only required flag exits non-zero with a clear wrapped error message (no stack trace).

Done when: 'npm test' runs this file and all cases pass.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: [test] CLI CRUD subcommands end-to-end
description:
Integration test verifying all five CRUD subcommands end-to-end against the in-memory NoteAPI.

Framework: Jest + supertest for the API layer; invoke CLI command handlers directly (import from src/cli/commands/*) with a mocked/base-URL-injected client, OR spawn the CLI via child_process against a test server on PORT=3001. Prefer importing handlers and asserting on captured stdout/stderr and exit code to avoid a live server.

File to create: tests/cli-crud.test.ts

Assertions (each must pass/fail clearly):
- create with --title/--content returns exit 0 and prints an id.
- list prints the created note; list --tag and --q filter correctly.
- read --id prints the matching note; read with unknown id exits non-zero and prints a wrapped { error } message (no stack trace).
- update --id changes the note and exits 0; update unknown id exits non-zero.
- delete --id removes the note (subsequent read fails); delete unknown id exits non-zero.

Done when: 'npm test' runs tests/cli-crud.test.ts and all cases pass.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: CLI CRUD commands (list/read/create/update/delete)
description: Implement five subcommands against the NoteAPI: list (optional --tag/--q filter), read (--id required), create (--title, --content required), update (--id required, --title/--content optional), delete (--id required). Each command calls the API and prints results to stdout. Non-zero exit on API error.

title: CLI help system and input validation
description: The CLI must support --help/-h flags globally and per subcommand, printing usage and exiting 0. Input validation must reject empty or whitespace-only arguments with a clear error message and non-zero exit code. No stack traces in error output.

title: Add --version flag to CLI
description: The CLI tool should support a --version (or -v) flag that prints the current version string and exits with code 0. Acceptance: running ./tool --version prints fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0, exit code 0, works alongside other flags.

title: [impl] Add --version/-v flag to CLI
description:
Add a --version (and -v) flag to the CLI.

Files to change:
- src/cli/version.ts (new) — export a VERSION constant read from package.json ('version' field) and printVersion() that writes 'fleet-e2e-toy v<version>' to stdout. Per gh-toy-4ef acceptance the string format is 'fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0' (package.json version is currently 1.0.0).
- src/cli/index.ts — when --version or -v is present, print the version line to stdout and exit 0, before command dispatch. Works alongside other flags (version short-circuits).

Note: -v is used for version here per the feature spec; ensure it does not collide with any other short flag in the arg parser.

Done when:
- 'noteapi --version' prints 'fleet-e2e-toy v1.0.0' and exits 0.
- 'noteapi -v' behaves identically.
- npm run build and npm run lint pass.

Part of feature gh-toy-4ef.

title: [impl] Add --help/-h help system to CLI (global and per-subcommand)
description:
Implement the CLI help system.

Files to change:
- src/cli/help.ts (new) — export printGlobalHelp() and printCommandHelp(command). Global help lists all subcommands (list, read, create, update, delete) and global flags (--help/-h, --version/-v). Per-command help shows the command's required/optional flags and a usage line.
- src/cli/index.ts — when --help or -h is present with no command, print global help to stdout and exit 0. When present with a command, print that command's help and exit 0. Unknown command prints a hint plus global usage to stderr and exits non-zero.

Behaviour:
- 'noteapi --help' and 'noteapi -h' print usage and exit 0.
- 'noteapi create --help' prints create usage and exits 0.
- Help output goes to stdout (exit 0); errors/usage-on-error go to stderr (non-zero exit). No stack traces.

Done when:
- Global and per-subcommand help work and exit 0.
- npm run build and npm run lint pass.

Part of feature gh-toy-7rp.

title: [impl] Implement create, update, and delete CLI subcommands
description:
Implement the write-side CRUD subcommands in the CLI.

Files to change:
- src/cli/commands/create.ts — requires --title and --content; optional --tag (repeatable or comma-separated). POST /api/notes. Prints created note id to stdout, exits 0. On API 400 validation error, prints wrapped { error } message to stderr, exits non-zero.
- src/cli/commands/update.ts — requires --id; optional --title/--content/--tag. PUT /api/notes/:id. Prints updated note to stdout. 404 -> non-zero exit with wrapped error.
- src/cli/commands/delete.ts — requires --id. DELETE /api/notes/:id. On 204 prints confirmation to stdout, exits 0. 404 -> non-zero exit with wrapped error.
- Wire all three into src/cli/index.ts dispatch.

Uses the client from src/cli/client.ts (gh-toy-ecn).

Done when:
- create/update/delete each call the correct endpoint and set a non-zero exit code on API error.
- No stack traces leak to stderr; errors are wrapped as { error: message }.
- npm run build and npm run lint pass.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=L model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=2800 reviewer_tokens=1232 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Implement list and read CLI subcommands
description:
Implement the read-side CRUD subcommands in the CLI.

Files to change:
- src/cli/commands/list.ts — 'list' subcommand. Optional --tag and --q filters passed as query params to GET /api/notes. Prints each note (id + title) to stdout, one per line. Empty result prints nothing and exits 0.
- src/cli/commands/read.ts — 'read' subcommand. Requires --id. Calls GET /api/notes/:id. Prints the note (id, title, content, tags) to stdout. On 404 prints '{ error: "Note not found" }' style message to stderr and exits non-zero.
- Wire both into src/cli/index.ts dispatch.

Uses the client from src/cli/client.ts (gh-toy-ecn).

Done when:
- 'noteapi list' prints all notes; 'noteapi list --tag work' filters by tag; 'noteapi list --q term' searches.
- 'noteapi read --id <id>' prints the note; missing/invalid id exits non-zero with a wrapped error message (no stack trace).
- npm run build and npm run lint pass.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: [impl] Scaffold CLI entry point, arg parser, and NoteAPI HTTP client
description:
Create the CLI foundation that all subcommands build on.

Files to create:
- src/cli/index.ts — CLI entry point. Reads process.argv, dispatches to the correct subcommand handler, sets process.exitCode on error. Add a bin/package.json script 'cli' -> 'ts-node src/cli/index.ts'.
- src/cli/client.ts — thin HTTP client wrapping fetch/http against the NoteAPI base URL (default http://localhost:3000, override via API_URL env). Exports functions: listNotes(params), getNote(id), createNote(input), updateNote(id, input), deleteNote(id). Each returns parsed JSON or throws a typed CliError with a message and suggested exit code on non-2xx.
- src/cli/args.ts — a small arg parser that turns argv into { command: string, flags: Record<string,string|boolean>, positional: string[] }. Support --flag value and --flag=value forms.

Conventions: No 'any' types (use interfaces from src/models/note.ts). No console.log in library code except the CLI's own stdout output layer. Wrap errors as { error: message } style, never raw error objects.

Done when:
- src/cli/index.ts, src/cli/client.ts, src/cli/args.ts exist and compile via 'npm run build'.
- 'npm run lint' passes for the new files (no any, no unused).
- Running the CLI with an unknown command prints a usage hint to stderr and exits non-zero.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=L model=claude-opus-4-8 doer_tokens=2800 reviewer_tokens=1232 output_usd=NaN

title: Add input validation for empty or blank strings
description:
Add shared input validation to the CLI so subcommands reject empty or whitespace-only argument values with a clear error and non-zero exit code.

Files to change:
- src/cli/validation.ts (new) — export validateRequired(name, value): throws CliError('<name> must be a non-empty string') when value is undefined, empty, or whitespace-only. Reuse the trim semantics from src/utils/validation.ts conventions.
- Wire validateRequired into create/read/update/delete handlers for their required flags (--title, --content, --id).

Behaviour:
- 'noteapi create --title "   " --content x' rejects with a clear message and non-zero exit, no stack trace.
- Error output is wrapped ({ error: message } style), never a raw Error object or stack trace.

Done when:
- Passing empty/blank required args exits non-zero with a readable message.
- A unit test in tests/cli-validation.test.ts covers empty string and whitespace-only cases.
- npm run build and npm run lint pass.

This task is part of feature gh-toy-7rp (CLI help system and input validation). Ref: gh-toy-v6z.
notes: cost-estimate: bucket=M model=claude-sonnet-4-6 doer_tokens=1400 reviewer_tokens=616 output_usd=NaN

title: Add config file support (~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml)
description: Allow users to persist default flag values in a YAML config file at ~/.fleet-e2e-toy.yaml so they don't have to repeat common flags on every run. Acceptance: reads config on startup, CLI flags override config, warns on unknown keys, documents supported keys in README.

title: Handle SIGINT gracefully (Ctrl-C)
description: The tool should catch SIGINT (Ctrl-C) and exit cleanly without a Python traceback or incomplete output. Acceptance: Ctrl-C prints 'Interrupted.' and exits with code 130, no stack trace shown, partial output files cleaned up.

title: Add JSON output mode via --json flag
description: Add a --json flag so all command output can be emitted as machine-readable JSON for scripting and CI pipelines. Acceptance: --json flag accepted on any subcommand, output is valid JSON, human-readable output is default, errors also JSON-formatted.

title: Tag filtering endpoint
description: GET /api/notes?tag=work returns only notes with that tag. Already partially implemented - needs tests.
